title: vaccinium urceolatum
intro: Vaccinium urceolatum, also known as the bell huckle, is a type of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Vaccinium urceolatum include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Vaccinium urceolatum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
